meta.name: BSMAS Bergen Social Media Addiction Scale
meta.description: The BSMAS is a 6-item brief scale assessing problematic social media use based on core components of addiction (salience, mood modification, tolerance, withdrawal, conflict, relapse).
meta.author: Andreassen et al. (2016)
meta.reference: Andreassen CS, et al. Development of a Facebook Addiction Scale. Psychological Reports. 2012;110(2):501-517.
meta.instruction: How often during the last year have you experienced the following related to your social media use (e.g., Instagram, TikTok, Twitter, Facebook)?

questions.text: Spent a lot of time thinking about social media or planning use of social media
Felt an urge to use social media more and more
Used social media in order to forget about personal problems
Tried to cut down on the use of social media without success
Become restless or troubled if you have been prohibited from using social media
Used social media so much that it has had a negative impact on your job/studies

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your social media use is within a healthy range.
suggestions: Continue maintaining moderate social media usage habits
Be mindful of your screen time

ranges.label: Mild Risk
ranges.description: You may have some unhealthy social media usage patterns.
suggestions: Set daily time limits for social media use
Develop offline social activities and hobbies

ranges.label: Moderate Risk
ranges.description: Social media use may already be affecting your daily life and interpersonal relationships.
suggestions: Reflect on how social media affects your mood
If you feel distressed, consider consulting a professional

ranges.label: High Risk
ranges.description: Your social media usage pattern has reached a problematic level and may be seriously affecting daily functioning.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to consult a mental health professional
Create a detailed usage restriction plan
Consider temporarily uninstalling your most-used social media apps
